author: kyrbies_DE
title: CloudBeats Konkurrent?
body:
Wirkt wie ein Evermusic Pro Clon mit Limitierung auf einen einzigen Cloud-Dienst?!

Der Player ist im Vergleich zu Bound und Marvis Pro keine Augenweide, aber die einzige Alternative zu CloudBeats, wenn man seine Musik aus der Cloud streamen möchte… wobei es hier auf Google Drive beschränkt ist, geteilte Ablagen funktionieren - auch ohne Erstellung einer Verknüpfung in „Meine Ablage“.

Persönlich bevorzuge ich CloudBeats audio book (mit MultiCloud) für Hörbücher von OneDrive und Google Drive da mir die Darstellung besser gefällt.

Das einzige was mir hier besser gefällt ist der automatische (Hintergrund) Sync der Meta-Daten, aber leider sehr langsam. Bei CloudBeats muss man den Sync immer manuell starten, damit die App auf neue Inhalte prüft.

Weil es aber scheinbar die einzige CloudBeats Alternative ist unterstütze ich die App gerne mit 4 Sternen, denn technisch funktioniert alles wie es soll und setzt auch beim harten schließen der App dann wieder dort fort wo man war…

title: Biblioteca, Otimização, e botões
body: A biblioteca de músicas não consegue carregar todas de forma “instantânea” ou “otimizada” falta melhorar muito essa parte, as músicas demoram MUITO para carregar e sempre tem que ficar “atualizando”  e esperando a página da biblioteca carregar todas as músicas, percebi que ela só consegue carregar 200 músicas de início depois disso demora muito. No modo aleatório os botões de “próxima” e “anterior” não escolhem uma próxima música aleatória ele seleciona a próxima música logo em baixo ou acima dependendo dos botões, um erro básico que já devia estar resolvido. E seria bem útil ter um sistema de reconhecimento de músicas repetidas para não tocar a mesma duas vezes seguidas.

I don’t want to download the files to my iPad. It was great that I was able to connect to my Google drive folders. The player works great to play one mp3 file, but:

1)  When  the song finishes, it seems to start playing the next one, which is good, but the app then crashes after a few seconds. I was hoping to be able to play all the songs in the “album” folder.

2) Ideally, I would like to be able to choose the  “All Albums” folder on my Google drive and have the app play recursively through each of the sub-folders.

author: Trizzle002
title: Horrible for local music files
body: I’m surprised by the glowing reviews.  I bought this to be able to synch my mp3s with google drive as well as have a library of local mp3s.  Every time I open the player it goes through a very lengthy process that seems to involve removing the track number from each song’s title, e.g., “01 I can’t get no satisfaction” becomes “I can’t get no satisfaction.”  Attempting to play any song having the track number in its title (before it’s been removed) causes it to freeze, with an “X” inside of a circle displayed over the title that won’t go away.  Also, every time I open it most songs (all local) are grayed out and can’t be played until the process completes.  The UI presents the choice of showing “online files” “local files” and “on my IPhone.”  What is the difference between “local files” and “on my IPhone?”  When I click the latter and browse to what is an mp3 on my iPhone, the “add to library” option does nothing, and there is no way to select the file or folder.  This is unusable!

title: Passable for Large Collections of 100k+ songs
body: I will start by saying I haven’t found anything better for the size of my collection. Apple and Google/YouTube both cap at 100,000 songs for their cloud music player and I got tired of the limit. This app solved my problem, at least in small part by lifting the cap. That said, it took over a week to process the metadata for all of my files and I eventually had to delete all of the album artwork because it was filling up my phone even at the smallest image size. If your collection is smaller and loads faster/fits with album art, this is a great player. If you are searching because you are over the 100k songs iTunes Match allows, give it a try but you’ll want to keep looking at the same time.

author: Stef J.
title: The ultimate app for mp3s!
body:
I don’t normally pay for apps, but $2.99 is a fair price to unlock GoPlayer’s full functionality. I am a Gen X music lover with a lot of CDs I am working on digitizing so I can enjoy my entire music collection anytime, anywhere. I certainly couldn’t afford to buy all those albums all over again in digital format. I’m using iTunes on an old laptop to rip the CDs to mp3 format and then uploading them to my Google Drive. GoPlayer makes it easy to create unlimited playlists and organize my music on my iPhone how I want. I don’t have to take up space on my phone to download the songs to listen to them unless I want to play them somewhere where there’s no WiFi, like in my car, through the Bluetooth stereo. 
I really like that the app somehow knows and automatically displays the song title, artist name, and album cover artwork for each song. This is a great feature that other apps don’t have. If the artwork is incorrect or you want to change it, which happens about 5% of the time, just upload the image you want to use on your Google Drive and you can access it on the app. The only thing that would make this app perfect is if you could order your playlists how you want. I would order mine alphabetically by artist name. Right now, they are listed in order of their creation or modification, from most to least recent, and you can’t change it. Overall, I would recommend this app.

***Edit: Someone must have read my review and modified the app accordingly because I noticed the other day that now my playlists are automatically alphabetized, which is fantastic, because it makes it easy to find what I want instead of aimlessly scrolling. Now the app is basically perfect so I’m upgrading my rating from 4 to 5 stars. ;) ***

title: Great but could use some metadata fixes
body: I’m really enjoying the app as a way to manage my Google Drive music library. Being able to play ALAC files in particular is great. The only area where I’d like to see some improvements is the way it handles a few metadata features. It doesn’t seem to manage multi-disc albums very well. Instead of sorting songs by disc and then by track, the entire set is sequenced by track. For example, the tracks will show up as D1-T1, D2-T1, D1-T2, D2-T2, etc. instead of D1-T1, D1-T2, D1-T3, etc. Another thing that would be nice to see is the option to group compilation artists together rather than having to scroll past artists with only one or two songs in my library.

author: DumbBut
title: Can’t change titles
body: Ok so I know this doesn’t seem like that big of a deal, however, it gets really frustrating. The only way I have managed to get music to google drive, sets it with random letters and numbers, so I have to go onto google drive to change it. This may seem simple however it gets rather difficult when I miss name some of them, then have to search all over my google drive account to find the song, listen to it to figure out what the actual title is of it, delete previous song from this app, then replace it with the proper one, all just so I don’t get mixed up on which song is which....other than that it’s good...other than the fact that the songs fade out as you get to the end of them, before they actually end.

author: FMCJ10
title: Fantastic Music App!
body:
I have been using Boom: Music Player & Equalizer for quite awhile. My iPhone is mostly for work related activities, yet I often found myself with time to listen to songs. I found this app reading about its features online, I decided to try it and I am very impressed. I like the value of the application, in comparison to Boom this is a winner and should only get better. Seriously, listening to my music collection via Google Drive is like being in heaven with the limited space Apple offers.

Suggestions:

3D sounds (Very popular, it makes music sound more dynamic and I really do miss this from the Boom app)

Song fading (Allowing is to choose how songs fade can give it that further edge, jukeboxes commonly have this feature and it allows tracks to flow nicely into each other - it’s also enjoyable via the radio when stations cross over music with a nice fade into the next song).

These are my two wishes, I know it’s a lot to ask for such a very reasonable app at such a low price but I think they would enhance this wonderful app.

Thank you for creating such a brilliant music player.

title: Great
body: I have been searching for forever for an app that will play music and download from drive. The last few apps all crashed or didn’t work properly since I had too many songs. I have them on my laptop but my laptop is a snowflake and requires its own special cable to connect with a phone. I can’t be bothered to get the cable and for some reason I can’t connect my two iTunes solely online. Basically this app is a lifesaver. You do need to upgrade to have more than 500 songs in a queue at one time but it’s cheap. Also the downloads are unlimited even if you don’t have an upgrade. You can just deal with creating different genre playlists so all your songs see the light. The downloading is excellent since it doesn’t cancel loading all of a sudden when your internet is at a weaker point.
